attacking_style: Launches attacks on descents and technical sections rather than summit finishes, often reading opponent weakness mid-stage. Historically attacked base of climbs (Mortirolo 2019) but shifted to descent/flat exploitation by career end (Lombardia 2017 lakeside attack 17km post-descent).
team_tactics: Frequently isolated without domestique support in decisive moments, forcing solo efforts. Multiple observations show numerical weakness (Astana 2022 Mortirolo, Bahrain weak TTT depth 2018). Relies on individual tactical reading rather than team-orchestrated setups.
terrain_preference: Descents remain elite weapon throughout career (2017 Bormio stage win, 2021 Giro descent attack). Technical positioning and corners favored over pure climbing power. Struggles on shallow/biathlon-circuit finishes and sustained high-tempo climbs where recovery matters more than explosiveness.
vulnerability: Time trials expose major weakness (lost 2min to Froome 2017 Vuelta, slower than Higuita 2020 Paris-Nice). Dropped early on climbs when lacking form (2-5km from summit repeatedly 2020-2022). Uneven pacing suggests declining recovery capacity mid-stage (2020 Giro tri-phase pattern). Unable to sustain efforts when isolated.
key_patterns: Descent attacks as primary weapon: 2017 Bormio win, 2021 Giro descent gain, 2019 Civiglio bridge (4+ instances)
Frequently dropped 2-5km from summit when out of form: 2021/2022 Giro, 2019 Tour, 2017 Angliru (6+ instances)
Isolated without team support in critical moments: 2022 Mortirolo, 2017 Vuelta Angliru, multiple Giro stages (5+ instances)
Aborts or softens attacks mid-effort when reading insufficient advantage: 2020 Giro visual assessment abort, 2018 Tour passivity
Contests sprint bonuses and positioning even when not attacking: 2017 Vuelta intermediate sprints, 2020 Paris-Nice gains
Clear decline trajectory 2020-2022: repeatedly distanced early climbs including home Sicily stage, career-ending signals evident
form_trajectory: negative: Clear decline from 2020 onward with repeated early distancing on climbs (Sicily home stage 2022, dropped sub-5km repeatedly). 2019 Tour Val Thorens win was outlier amid growing weakness signals. Retired after 2022 Giro showed inability to defend positions.
